title: Where did "resting is wasting time" come from?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for tracing the origin of rest guilt.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 13 of the SLEEP program. The prompts ask where the belief that rest must be earned first appeared — who taught it, what it cost, and whether it is actually yours.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 13 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795), "Let Go of Guilt for Rest."

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to understand where your rest guilt came from before trying to shift it — particularly useful early in a programme.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 13 of the SLEEP program. The prompts trace the origin of rest guilt: who taught you that stopping was wasting, what you gave up to follow that rule, and whether you ever chose to adopt it or just inherited it. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What does my inner voice say when I rest without "earning" it first?
Write the exact words. The tone. Does it sound like your own voice or someone else's?
How do I decide whether I've "done enough" to deserve rest?
Is there a list? A feeling? A moment of permission? Or is it never quite enough?
Who in my early life modelled that resting was lazy or indulgent?
Name the person or the household. What did they do or say that taught you the rule?
What has following this rule actually cost me, in terms of sleep, health, and pleasure?
Write specific losses — seasons of exhaustion, things you missed, what your body has paid.
If I rewrote the rule, what would it say instead?
Write a new standard for rest in your life. Not what it should say but what you'd actually choose if you were writing it from scratch.
Adapted from Day 13 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
